title: Acuerdo de distribución de dividendo ordinario
summary: Board approved ordinary dividend of CLP 850 per share, payable 15 May 2025.
aiSummary: SQM declared an ordinary dividend of CLP 850/share (~2.7% yield), payable May 15. This is positive for income-oriented holders but note it occurs against a backdrop of significant earnings pressure from falling lithium prices. The dividend is supported by cash accumulated in prior high-price periods. Management appears confident enough to maintain the distribution policy despite YoY earnings compression, which may signal they see a lithium price floor forming.
keyPoints: Dividend: CLP 850/share, payable 15 May 2025
Implied yield ~2.7% at current price
Dividend maintained despite Q1 2025 earnings weakness (-45% YoY net income)
Signal of management confidence in cash position despite lithium headwinds

title: Colocación de bonos en el mercado local
summary: Banco Santander Chile placed UF 5 million in senior bonds at 2.80% real rate, 10-year term.
aiSummary: Routine liability management. Santander Chile issued UF 5MM in 10-year senior bonds at 2.80% real rate. At current UF values this is approximately CLP 192 billion (~USD 205MM). The real rate is consistent with recent market clearing levels for investment-grade bank paper. This transaction diversifies the funding maturity profile and locks in current favorable long-term rates ahead of further BCCh easing. Neutral to earnings short-term.
keyPoints: UF 5 million senior bonds, 10-year term at 2.80% real rate
Approximately CLP 192 billion equivalent
Routine liability management; extends maturity profile
Consistent with market rates for IG bank paper; neutral to near-term earnings

title: Designación de nuevo Gerente de Finanzas
summary: Falabella announced appointment of a new CFO effective 1 April 2025, after 8 years with the outgoing CFO.
aiSummary: CFO change after an 8-year tenure. The timing coincides with Falabella's ongoing margin recovery effort and e-commerce profitability push. New CFO leadership could bring fresh capital allocation priorities. Investors should monitor whether the transition introduces any delay to the financial restructuring plan or alters the pace of debt reduction. Management continuity in core commercial operations mitigates transition risk. Impact is neutral unless there is a shift in financial strategy communicated at the next earnings call.
keyPoints: New CFO effective 1 April 2025; outgoing CFO after 8-year tenure
Transition during active margin recovery and e-commerce profitability initiative
Watch next earnings call for any shift in capital allocation or debt reduction strategy
Operational leadership remains unchanged; commercial risk is low

title: Aprobación de aumento de capital por refinanciamiento de deuda
summary: Extraordinary shareholders meeting approved capital increase of up to USD 500 million to fund debt refinancing.
aiSummary: High-materiality event. CMPC's USD 500MM capital raise (pro-rata rights offering) targets debt refinancing, not growth capex. This signals the balance sheet is under pressure from pulp price weakness, and management is prioritizing deleveraging over distribution. The dilutive impact is material — existing shareholders who do not participate will see their stake reduced. However, if successful, the raise reduces refinancing risk and extends the debt maturity profile. Monitor take-up rate and pricing when the rights offering terms are announced.
keyPoints: Up to USD 500MM capital increase via pro-rata rights offering to existing shareholders
Proceeds earmarked for debt refinancing, not new investment
Balance sheet pressure signal from sustained pulp price weakness
Dilutive to non-participating shareholders; watch take-up rate and pricing
Positive if deleveraging reduces interest burden; negative short-term for EPS

title: Acuerdo de venta de participación en activos termoeléctricos
summary: Enel Chile agreed to sell 49% of remaining thermal generation assets for USD 180 million.
aiSummary: Strategic asset disposal consistent with Enel Chile's renewables-first transition. Selling 49% of thermal assets at USD 180MM implies a valuation that looks reasonable given stranded-asset risk in thermal generation. Net proceeds strengthen the balance sheet for renewable investment and reduce carbon exposure. The remaining 51% stake means Enel Chile retains operational control. Subject to regulatory approval (Q3 2025 expected). Positive catalyst: reduces legacy thermal drag on EBITDA margins and funds renewable capex without dilution.
keyPoints: 49% stake in thermal assets sold to local infrastructure fund for USD 180MM
Regulatory approval expected Q3 2025; cash receipt likely H2 2025
Reduces thermal generation exposure; accelerates renewables transition
Enel Chile retains 51% — operational control maintained
USD 180MM proceeds to fund renewable investment pipeline

title: Adquisición de empresa acuícola en Noruega
summary: Copec's subsidiary Blumar completed acquisition of a Norwegian aquaculture company for USD 320 million.
aiSummary: Material acquisition — USD 320MM for European aquaculture expansion through Blumar. Size is significant relative to Copec's market cap (~2% of value). The deal adds geographic diversification to salmon farming but brings execution risk: Norwegian regulatory environment, currency (NOK), and integration costs. Salmon prices are cyclical and currently at moderate levels. Key questions: implied EV/EBITDA multiple, integration timeline, and leverage impact on Copec's consolidated balance sheet. Neutral to negative near-term on uncertainty; positive long-term if salmon cycle turns.
keyPoints: USD 320MM acquisition of Norwegian aquaculture company via Blumar subsidiary
Expands salmon farming into European waters; geographic diversification
Material size relative to Copec's consolidated balance sheet
Key risk: Norwegian regulatory environment and currency (NOK) exposure
Watch: integration costs, implied acquisition multiple, leverage impact

title: Emisión de bonos subordinados para capital Tier 2
summary: BCI placed CLP 120,000 million in 15-year subordinated bonds at 5.95% to qualify as Tier 2 capital.
aiSummary: Routine capital management. CLP 120BN (~USD 128MM) in 15-year subordinated bonds qualifies as Tier 2 capital under CMF Basel III implementation. This improves BCI's Total Capital Adequacy Ratio without diluting equity holders. At 5.95%, the coupon is consistent with market rates for subordinated bank paper. Negative carry versus the loan book is minimal given current rates. The issuance supports BCI's ability to grow the loan book within regulatory capital constraints. Neutral to slightly positive for ROE if capital is deployed efficiently.
keyPoints: CLP 120,000MM in 15-year subordinated Tier 2 bonds at 5.95%
Strengthens Total Capital Adequacy Ratio under CMF Basel III
Non-dilutive to equity holders; supports loan book growth headroom
Coupon at market rate; neutral to near-term earnings

title: Actualización de guidance de capacidad y márgenes 2025
summary: LATAM raised FY2025 capacity guidance to ASK +8% YoY (from +6-8%); EBITDAR margin target 17-19% maintained.
aiSummary: Positive guidance revision. LATAM raised capacity growth to the top of the prior range (+8% vs +6-8%), driven by strong demand and fuel tailwinds. EBITDAR margin target maintained at 17-19% — important because higher capacity with flat margins implies improving unit economics. The guidance update signals management confidence in the demand environment and cost discipline post-restructuring. For investors, the key watch item is whether cargo yield normalization offsets passenger strength in H2. Stock positive on raised guidance; risk is fuel price reversal.
keyPoints: ASK capacity guidance raised to +8% YoY (from +6-8% range)
EBITDAR margin target maintained at 17-19% — positive signal for unit economics
Strong passenger demand and lower fuel costs cited as tailwinds
Watch: cargo yield normalization and fuel price sensitivity in H2 2025

title: Reclamo ambiental contra planta de Huasco
summary: Environmental authority filed complaint against CAP's Huasco plant for dust emissions; potential fine up to USD 2MM.
aiSummary: Environmental risk event. Formal complaint by SERNAGEOMIN/SMA against the Huasco pelletizing plant for dust emission exceedance. Fine exposure (up to USD 2MM) is financially immaterial for CAP. More important is whether the complaint could escalate to a production halt or license suspension — the filing explicitly states no production halt was ordered. CAP has 30 days to respond. The environmental scrutiny comes at a difficult time given existing earnings pressure from iron ore prices. Monitor for escalation or formal sanction proceedings.
keyPoints: Formal complaint for dust emissions exceeding permitted limits at Huasco plant
Fine exposure up to USD 2MM — financially immaterial
No production halt ordered; 30 days to respond
Risk: escalation to formal sanction or license conditions
Context: CAP already under pressure from iron ore <USD 100/t

title: Aprobación de dividendo trimestral
summary: Board approved quarterly dividend of CLP 2.50/share, payable 10 May 2025.
aiSummary: Routine quarterly dividend, CLP 2.50/share payable May 10. Reflects the strong operating cash flow generation of Parque Arauco's diversified mall portfolio across Chile, Peru, and Colombia. The dividend policy signals confidence in recurring NOI and stabilized occupancy rates. For REITs/real estate operators, consistent dividend cadence is a key investor comfort metric. Slightly positive — reinforces Parque Arauco's income investment thesis.
keyPoints: Quarterly dividend: CLP 2.50/share, payable 10 May 2025
Supported by strong NOI from Chile, Peru, Colombia mall portfolio
Consistent quarterly cadence reinforces income investment thesis
Occupancy and same-property NOI growth remain key metrics to monitor

title: Operación entre partes relacionadas — préstamo a Cencosud Shopping
summary: Cencosud extended USD 50MM intercompany loan to subsidiary Cencosud Shopping at 6.2% annual, 3-year term.
aiSummary: Related-party transaction filed for transparency. USD 50MM intercompany loan from Cencosud S.A. to Cencosud Shopping (real estate subsidiary) at 6.2% annual, 3-year term. Independent director approval and CMF related-party protocols applied. The rate appears arms-length given current credit market conditions. The transaction supports Cencosud Shopping's working capital needs within the group structure. No minority shareholder rights concern given procedural compliance. Neutral — routine for diversified conglomerate treasury management.
keyPoints: USD 50MM intercompany loan to Cencosud Shopping at 6.2%, 3-year term
Independent director approval obtained; CMF related-party protocol followed
Rate appears market-consistent; procedural compliance reduces governance risk
Neutral to consolidated earnings (intragroup elimination)

title: SUBTEL autoriza expansión de espectro 5G en regiones del norte
summary: SUBTEL granted Entel 5G spectrum expansion in northern regions covering 2.1 million additional subscribers.
aiSummary: Strategic positive. SUBTEL authorization to expand 5G into Atacama, Antofagasta, and Tarapacá adds ~2.1 million potential subscribers in mining-intensive regions with higher ARPU potential. Mining company connectivity is a premium B2B segment. Deployment begins Q3 2025. Near-term capex impact (~CLP 15-20 billion estimated) is manageable; long-term revenue upside meaningful. This strengthens Entel's competitive position in B2B/enterprise connectivity vs Movistar and WOM. Positive for Entel's premium positioning thesis.
keyPoints: 5G spectrum authorized for Atacama, Antofagasta, Tarapacá regions
2.1 million additional potential subscribers; mining-B2B premium opportunity
Deployment starts Q3 2025; capex manageable at ~CLP 15-20BN estimated
Strengthens enterprise/B2B competitive position vs Movistar and WOM

title: Reafirmación de metas de rentabilidad para 2025
summary: Banco de Chile management confirmed ROE target of 20-22% for FY2025 at investor day.
aiSummary: Constructive investor day. Banco de Chile reaffirmed 20-22% ROE target for FY2025, maintaining best-in-class profitability guidance in the Chilean banking sector. Management acknowledged NIM compression from further rate cuts is expected but flagged volume growth and fee income as offsets. The 20-22% ROE range implies continued strong absolute earnings even under rate pressure. This is a comfort statement ahead of H2 results. The stock typically trades on ROE premium to sector; target confirmation supports current valuation multiples. Positive tone with manageable downside risk.
keyPoints: ROE target 20-22% for FY2025 reaffirmed at investor day
Best-in-class profitability in Chilean banking sector
NIM compression from BCCh cuts offset by volume growth and fee income
Premium ROE guidance supports current valuation multiple

title: Adquisición de empresa de servicios TI en Brasil
summary: SONDA acquired 70% of a Brazilian IT services company for USD 45 million; call option for remaining 30% in 2027.
aiSummary: Meaningful Brazilian expansion. USD 45MM for 70% of a cloud infrastructure specialist adds scale in Brazil — SONDA's key growth market. 850 specialized employees enhance delivery capacity for enterprise cloud contracts. The 30% call option in 2027 provides a path to full ownership at a likely step-up. The acquisition multiple (implied ~8-10x EBITDA estimated) is in line with IT services peers. Key integration risk: SONDA has mixed track record on Latin American M&A execution. Monitor H2 2025 results for revenue synergy evidence.
keyPoints: 70% stake acquired for USD 45MM; cloud infrastructure specialist, 850 employees
Call option for remaining 30% in 2027 at step-up price
Brazil is SONDA's key growth market; adds enterprise cloud delivery capacity
Watch: integration execution and revenue synergy timing in H2 2025

title: Venta de dos buques portacontenedores
summary: CSAV completed sale of two container vessels (24,000 TEU total) for USD 58 million, with capital gain of ~USD 12 million.
aiSummary: Asset optimization in normalization cycle. CSAV sold two vessels (12,000 TEU each) for USD 58MM, generating ~USD 12MM capital gain. The decision to sell at these prices reflects management's view that container freight rates have stabilized at post-pandemic normalized levels and fleet optimization is preferable to holding aging assets. Proceeds may fund an extraordinary dividend or fleet renewal. CSAV's real value driver remains its 28% stake in Hapag-Lloyd — vessel sales are secondary. Dividend potential from proceeds is the key near-term catalyst.
keyPoints: Two 12,000 TEU vessels sold for USD 58MM total; ~USD 12MM capital gain
Container freight rates at normalized post-pandemic levels — asset recycling rational
Proceeds may fund extraordinary dividend or fleet renewal capex
CSAV's primary value driver is Hapag-Lloyd stake (~28%); vessel ops secondary

title: SQM — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue -32.5% YoY; EBITDA -48.2% YoY; net income -45.1% YoY. EBITDA margin 25%.
aiSummary: Weak quarter driven entirely by lithium carbonate price collapse. Revenue fell 32.5% YoY despite stable lithium volumes because spot and contract prices are down ~45% YoY. EBITDA margin compressed from ~37% to 25% — still industry-leading but the trajectory is concerning if prices don't stabilize. The key unknown is SQM's ongoing contract renegotiation with Codelco for the Atacama salar — terms could structurally reset the cost base. At current lithium prices, SQM trades on cyclical trough valuation. Recovery depends on battery supply chain normalization and EV demand acceleration in China.
keyPoints: Revenue: CLP 780,000MM (-32.5% YoY); primary driver is lithium price, not volume
EBITDA margin 25.0% vs 37.5% prior year — significant compression
Net income: CLP 115,000MM (-45.1% YoY)
Watch: Atacama contract renegotiation terms with Codelco
Lithium carbonate spot ~USD 12,800/t; recovery contingent on EV demand

title: Santander Chile — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +8.2% YoY; net income +11.5% YoY. Clean quarter — NIM expansion, stable credit quality.
aiSummary: Strong and clean quarter. Net income grew 11.5% YoY on NIM expansion driven by loan repricing at still-elevated real rates. Cost of risk held stable — no early signs of credit deterioration despite moderate consumer pressure. Fee income was flat YoY, which bears watching as rate cuts compress NII. Santander Chile's advantage vs peers is its lower wholesale funding reliance, which limits NIM sensitivity on the liability side. Near-term risk: further BCCh cuts will compress NIM; management guidance of moderate deceleration into H2 is the base case. Stock remains a quality compounder in Chilean banking.
keyPoints: Net income: CLP 195,000MM (+11.5% YoY) — clean beat
NIM expansion on loan repricing; credit quality stable
Fee income flat YoY — watch as primary offset to NIM compression from rate cuts
Lower wholesale funding base limits liability-side NIM sensitivity vs peers

title: Falabella — Resultados Q4 2024
summary: Q4 2024 revenue +4.8% YoY; EBITDA -2.1% YoY; net income -18.5% YoY. Mixed result — FX headwinds in region.
aiSummary: Mixed quarter with revenue recovery offset by margin pressure. Chile same-store sales +3.5% is constructive, but regional FX depreciation (ARS, COP) hit consolidated margins. E-commerce remained a drag on profitability despite growing GMV. EBITDA margin compressed to 12% — still meaningfully below the 14-16% range the business can generate in a normalized environment. New CFO (effective April 2025) may bring renewed focus on cost discipline. The key investment thesis remains: can Falabella restore margins while managing the e-commerce profitability ramp? Execution risk is elevated.
keyPoints: Revenue: CLP 2,850,000MM (+4.8% YoY); Chile SSS +3.5%
EBITDA margin 12.0% (vs 12.8% prior year) — FX and cost pressure
Net income -18.5% YoY; below consensus on higher FX losses
E-commerce profitability path and new CFO strategy are key Q1 2025 catalysts

title: Copec — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +6.1% YoY; EBITDA +12.4% YoY; net income +18.2% YoY. Clean — fuel and Arauco both contributed.
aiSummary: Clean quarter with broad-based strength. Fuel distribution margins held firm in Chile and international markets; Arauco (forestry/panels) contributed positively on improved timber and pulp pricing vs prior year. Net income grew 18.2% YoY — strong in absolute terms. The Norwegian aquaculture acquisition (Blumar) adds a new earnings stream but integration costs will appear in future quarters. Copec is a quality business with diversified exposure to Chilean energy demand and global forest products. Key near-term question: how does the Blumar integration impact consolidated margins in Q2-Q3 2025?
keyPoints: EBITDA: CLP 480,000MM (+12.4% YoY); margin 11.4% (+0.6pp)
Fuel distribution: stable margins in Chile and international ops
Arauco forestry: improved timber pricing vs prior year
Watch: Blumar (Norway) integration costs and capex in Q2-Q3 2025

title: Enel Chile — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +12.8% YoY; EBITDA +18.5% YoY; net income +22.4% YoY. EBITDA margin 36% — best-in-class.
aiSummary: Excellent quarter driven by renewables growth and hydrology recovery. Enel Chile's 36% EBITDA margin is best-in-class for Chilean utilities. Solar and wind additions drove revenue growth; improved hydrology (after a dry 2024) boosted output from hydroelectric assets. The 22.4% net income growth is impressive and ahead of consensus. Regulatory risk remains the primary overhang — the tariff review process could reset generation pricing in H2 2025. New renewable capacity commissioning schedule is also key. Enel Chile is the cleanest ESG-aligned utility play in Chile's equity market.
keyPoints: EBITDA margin: 36.0% (+1.8pp) — best-in-class for Chilean utilities
Net income: CLP 92,000MM (+22.4% YoY) — ahead of consensus
Drivers: solar/wind additions + hydrology normalization from dry 2024
Key risk: tariff review outcome (H2 2025) and commissioning delays on new capacity

title: LATAM Airlines — Resultados Q4 2024
summary: Q4 2024 revenue +9.2% YoY; EBITDA +14.1% YoY; net income +28.5% YoY. Strong demand + lower fuel = margin expansion.
aiSummary: Impressive quarter — best post-restructuring result to date. All traffic corridors showed strong demand; capacity +8% YoY. Lower Brent oil prices materially reduced fuel cost per ASK, driving EBITDAR margin to 18.1% — top of the 17-19% guidance range. Balance sheet has normalized post-Chapter 11; net debt/EBITDA approaching target leverage. The stock has re-rated significantly from restructuring levels. Going forward, key risks are cargo yield normalization and fleet renewal capex. Maintain watch on fuel hedging strategy for 2025.
keyPoints: EBITDAR margin 18.1% — top of guidance range; net income +28.5% YoY
Capacity +8% YoY; strong yield in all corridors
Fuel cost tailwind: lower Brent + favorable hedging
Balance sheet normalized; leverage trending toward mid-cycle target
Watch: cargo yield normalization and fleet renewal capex in 2025

title: Banco de Chile — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +6.8% YoY; net income +9.2% YoY. ROE >25% maintained. NII growth offset higher provisions.
aiSummary: Best-in-class ROE maintained above 25% — Banco de Chile continues to outperform sector peers on profitability. Loan portfolio grew 5% YoY with UF-indexed book benefiting from residual inflation pass-through. Higher provision expense (+15% YoY) reflects conservative coverage ratios in SME segment ahead of expected credit normalization. NIM was stable QoQ, which is positive given the rate environment. Banco de Chile's premium over sector (typically 15-20% P/BV premium) appears justified by ROE consistency. Watch asset quality deterioration in SME and mortgage book as economic recovery is still uneven.
keyPoints: Net income: CLP 188,000MM (+9.2% YoY); ROE >25% maintained
Loan growth +5% YoY; UF-indexed portfolio benefits from CPI pass-through
Provision expense +15% YoY — conservative approach in SME segment
Premium valuation justified by consistent ROE delivery; watch SME credit quality

title: BCI — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +5.1% YoY; net income +7.8% YoY. City National Bank (US) contributed positively.
aiSummary: Solid quarter, in line with expectations. BCI's differentiated asset — City National Bank (CNB) in the US — contributed positively, providing geographic diversification not seen in local peers. Chilean corporate loan book growing at 5%+ driven by infrastructure and mining projects. Controlled credit costs with no significant spike in NPLs. Key risk for BCI is CNB's exposure to US credit cycle: if Fed keeps rates high longer, commercial real estate and tech lending at CNB face headwinds. BCI trades at a discount to Santander Chile and Banco de Chile — narrowing of that discount is the re-rating catalyst if CNB performs well.
keyPoints: Net income: CLP 155,000MM (+7.8% YoY); in line with consensus
City National Bank (US) contribution positive — geographic diversification
Chile corporate loan book +5% driven by infrastructure/mining
Key risk: CNB exposure to US credit cycle; watch commercial real estate
